results.title: Prompt-RAG: Pioneering Vector Embedding-Free Retrieval-Augmented Generation in Niche Domains, Exemplified by Korean Medicine
results.tldr: Prompt-RAG, distinct from conventional RAG models, operates without the need for embedding vectors, showing that Prompt-RAG outperformed existing models, including ChatGPT and conventional vector embedding-based RAGs, in terms of relevance and informativeness.

results.tldr: Theoretical analysis and empirical results demonstrate the unique advantages of auto-regressive embedding generation in terms of training efficiency and scalability at test time and sheds light on the generative paradigm within multimodal document retrieval.

results.title: QDER: Query-Specific Document and Entity Representations for Multi-Vector Document Re-Ranking
results.venue: Proceedings of the 48th International ACM SIGIR Conference on Research and Development in Information Retrieval
results.abstract: QDER is a neural re-ranking model that unifies these approaches by integrating knowledge graph semantics into a multi-vector model that excels on difficult queries, setting a foundation for future work in entity-aware retrieval.
results.tldr: QDER is a neural re-ranking model that unifies these approaches by integrating knowledge graph semantics into a multi-vector model that excels on difficult queries, setting a foundation for future work in entity-aware retrieval.

results.title: Incorporating the vector space model in a neural network used for document retrieval
results.abstract: <jats:p>The task of a document retrieval system is to match a query, perhaps in natural language, against a large number of natural language documents. Neural networks are known to be good pattern matchers. This article describes investigations in implementing a document retrieval system based on a neural network model. It shows that many of the standard strategies of information retrieval are applicable in a neural network model.</jats:p>

results.title: Beyond the Grid: Layout-Informed Multi-Vector Retrieval with Parsed Visual Document Representations
results.tldr: ColParse is introduced, a novel paradigm that leverages a document parsing model to generate a small set of layout-informed sub-image embeddings, which are then fused with a global page-level vector to create a compact and structurally-aware multi-vector representation.
results.abstract: ColParse is introduced, a novel paradigm that leverages a document parsing model to generate a small set of layout-informed sub-image embeddings, which are then fused with a global page-level vector to create a compact and structurally-aware multi-vector representation.

results.title: Incorporating Token Importance in Multi-Vector Retrieval
Efficient Multi-Vector Dense Retrieval Using Bit Vectors
results.tldr: Efficient Multi-Vector dense retrieval with Bit vectors (EMVB), a novel framework for efficient query processing in multi-vector dense retrieval, which employs a highly efficient pre-filtering step of passages using optimized bit vectors and introduces a per-document term filtering method that further improves the efficiency of the last step.
results.abstract: Efficient Multi-Vector dense retrieval with Bit vectors (EMVB), a novel framework for efficient query processing in multi-vector dense retrieval, which employs a highly efficient pre-filtering step of passages using optimized bit vectors and introduces a per-document term filtering method that further improves the efficiency of the last step.

results.title: VILE: Block-Aware Visual Enhanced Document Retrieval
results.venue: International Conference on Information and Knowledge Management
results.tldr: This work proposes a new dense retrieval model, namely VILE, to incorporate visual features into document representations, and collects screenshots and the corresponding layout information of some web pages in the MS MARCO Document Ranking dataset, resulting in a new multi-modal document retrieval dataset.
results.abstract: This work proposes a new dense retrieval model, namely VILE, to incorporate visual features into document representations, and collects screenshots and the corresponding layout information of some web pages in the MS MARCO Document Ranking dataset, resulting in a new multi-modal document retrieval dataset.

results.title: Comparison of Vector Space Representations of Documents for the Task of Information Retrieval of Massive Open Online Courses
results.tldr: This article addresses the question of searching for relevant massive open online courses (MOOC) using a course programme document as a query using the application of language modelling.
results.abstract: This article addresses the question of searching for relevant massive open online courses (MOOC) using a course programme document as a query using the application of language modelling.

results.venue: 2017 3rd International Conference on Science in Information Technology (ICSITech)

results.title: Enhancing Security and Applicability of Local LLM-Based Document Retrieval Systems in Smart Grid Isolated Environments
results.tldr: This study presents a retrieval-augmented question-answering system designed to operate entirely offline, integrating local vector embeddings, ontology-based semantic enrichment, and quantized LLMs, while ensuring compliance with industrial security standards like IEC 62351.
results.abstract: This study presents a retrieval-augmented question-answering system designed to operate entirely offline, integrating local vector embeddings, ontology-based semantic enrichment, and quantized LLMs, while ensuring compliance with industrial security standards like IEC 62351.

results.title: Hierarchical Patch Compression for ColPali: Efficient Multi-Vector Document Retrieval with Dynamic Pruning and Quantization
results.venue: International Joint Conference on Knowledge Discovery, Knowledge Engineering and Knowledge Management
results.tldr: HPC-ColPali is proposed, a Hierarchical Patch Compression framework that enhances the efficiency of ColPali while preserving its retrieval accuracy and establishes HPC-ColPali as a scalable and efficient solution for multi-vector document retrieval across diverse applications.
results.abstract: HPC-ColPali is proposed, a Hierarchical Patch Compression framework that enhances the efficiency of ColPali while preserving its retrieval accuracy and establishes HPC-ColPali as a scalable and efficient solution for multi-vector document retrieval across diverse applications.

results.title: Learning Query Token Importance for Effective Document Retrieval with Verbose Queries
results.tldr: This work proposes a model that learns the importance of individual query tokens in verbose queries by leveraging the representations from the pre-trained query encoder of ColBERT, and assigns gold-standard labels for the tokens of training queries using an automatic annotation method leveraging the publicly available topics from NIST datasets.
results.abstract: This work proposes a model that learns the importance of individual query tokens in verbose queries by leveraging the representations from the pre-trained query encoder of ColBERT, and assigns gold-standard labels for the tokens of training queries using an automatic annotation method leveraging the publicly available topics from NIST datasets.

results.title: ReinPool: Reinforcement Learning Pooling Multi-Vector Embeddings for Retrieval System
results.tldr: ReinPool is introduced, a reinforcement learning framework that learns to dynamically filter and pool multi-vector embeddings into compact, retrieval-optimized representations by training with an inverse retrieval objective and NDCG-based rewards, demonstrating that learned selection significantly outperforms heuristic aggregation.
results.abstract: ReinPool is introduced, a reinforcement learning framework that learns to dynamically filter and pool multi-vector embeddings into compact, retrieval-optimized representations by training with an inverse retrieval objective and NDCG-based rewards, demonstrating that learned selection significantly outperforms heuristic aggregation.

results.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Quantized Large Language Models: A Comparative Analysis
results.tldr: The practical application in industrial settings, particularly in assisting design personnel with navigating complex standards and quality manuals, is discussed, and the findings suggest that 4-bit integer quantization is optimal for standard document retrieval and question-answering tasks, highlighting practical considerations for CPU inference.
results.abstract: The practical application in industrial settings, particularly in assisting design personnel with navigating complex standards and quality manuals, is discussed, and the findings suggest that 4-bit integer quantization is optimal for standard document retrieval and question-answering tasks, highlighting practical considerations for CPU inference.

results.title: MultiContrievers: Analysis of Dense Retrieval Representations
results.venue: BlackboxNLP Workshop on Analyzing and Interpreting Neural Networks for NLP
results.tldr: It is found that contriever models have significantly increased extractability, but extractability usually correlates poorly with benchmark performance, and there is high sensitivity to both random initialisation and to data shuffle, suggesting that future retrieval research should test across a wider spread of both.
results.abstract: It is found that contriever models have significantly increased extractability, but extractability usually correlates poorly with benchmark performance, and there is high sensitivity to both random initialisation and to data shuffle, suggesting that future retrieval research should test across a wider spread of both.

results.title: LEMUR: Learned Multi-Vector Retrieval
results.tldr: This work forms multi-vector similarity search as a supervised learning problem that can be solved using a one-hidden-layer neural network, and reduces inference under this model to single-vector similarity search in its latent space, enabling the use of existing single-vector search indexes to accelerate retrieval.
results.abstract: This work forms multi-vector similarity search as a supervised learning problem that can be solved using a one-hidden-layer neural network, and reduces inference under this model to single-vector similarity search in its latent space, enabling the use of existing single-vector search indexes to accelerate retrieval.

results.tldr: This study investigates the effectiveness of paragraph vector models to compute and utilize document-wise similarities of Turkish news articles and proposes an ensemble categorization approach that consists of three main stages, namely, document processing, paragraph vector learning, and document similarity estimation.
results.abstract: This study investigates the effectiveness of paragraph vector models to compute and utilize document-wise similarities of Turkish news articles and proposes an ensemble categorization approach that consists of three main stages, namely, document processing, paragraph vector learning, and document similarity estimation.

results.title: Interleaved Multi-Modal Document Representations for Large-Scale Information Retrieval Using Large Language Models
results.abstract: The exponential growth of multi-modal data has posed significant challenges for traditional information retrieval systems, which often struggle to effectively integrate and process content across different formats, such as text and images. A novel approach is introduced through a unified interleaved representation that enables the seamless integration of multi-modal inputs, allowing for more accurate and efficient retrieval of complex content. The method leverages cross-modal attention mechan

results.title: Evo-Retriever: LLM-Guided Curriculum Evolution with Viewpoint-Pathway Collaboration for Multimodal Document Retrieval
results.tldr: Evo-Retriever is introduced, a retrieval framework featuring an LLM-guided curriculum evolution built upon a novel Viewpoint-Pathway collaboration, which adaptively adjusts the training curriculum using expert knowledge to promote the model's evolution.
results.abstract: Evo-Retriever is introduced, a retrieval framework featuring an LLM-guided curriculum evolution built upon a novel Viewpoint-Pathway collaboration, which adaptively adjusts the training curriculum using expert knowledge to promote the model's evolution.

results.title: M3DR: Towards Universal Multilingual Multimodal Document Retrieval
results.tldr: A framework designed to bridge the gap across languages, enabling applicability across diverse linguistic and cultural contexts, and achieves state-of-the-art performance with ~150% relative improvements on cross-lingual retrieval.
results.abstract: A framework designed to bridge the gap across languages, enabling applicability across diverse linguistic and cultural contexts, and achieves state-of-the-art performance with ~150% relative improvements on cross-lingual retrieval.

results.title: EmBoost: Embedding Boosting to Learn Multilevel Abstract Text Representation for Document Retrieval
results.venue: International Conference on Agents and Artificial Intelligence
results.tldr: The multilevel abstract word embedding is consistently superior to existing solo strategies including Glove, FastText, ELMo and BERT-based models and the study shows that further gains can be made when a deep residual neural model is specifically trained for document retrieval.
results.abstract: The multilevel abstract word embedding is consistently superior to existing solo strategies including Glove, FastText, ELMo and BERT-based models and the study shows that further gains can be made when a deep residual neural model is specifically trained for document retrieval.

results.title: Intelligent PDF Query System for Document Analysis
results.venue: 2025 International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)

results.title: Multi-Vector Biomedical Dense Retrieval with Knowledge-Enhanced Entity-Type Clustering
results.venue: ACM Transactions on Knowledge Discovery from Data
results.tldr: ELK-Multi is proposed, a novel multi-vector retrieval framework that constructs fine-grained document representations through knowledge-enhanced entity-type clustering that establishes new state-of-the-art results in NDCG and Recall.
results.abstract: ELK-Multi is proposed, a novel multi-vector retrieval framework that constructs fine-grained document representations through knowledge-enhanced entity-type clustering that establishes new state-of-the-art results in NDCG and Recall.

results.title: A Unified Model and Document Representation for On-Device Retrieval-Augmented Generation
results.tldr: This approach represents the first model to unify retrieval and context compression using a shared model and representation and it is believed this work will inspire further consolidation of distinct models to optimize on-device performance.
results.abstract: This approach represents the first model to unify retrieval and context compression using a shared model and representation and it is believed this work will inspire further consolidation of distinct models to optimize on-device performance.

results.title: GEM: A Native Graph-based Index for Multi-Vector Retrieval
results.venue: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
results.tldr: GEM is presented, a native indexing framework for multi-vector representations to construct a proximity graph directly over vector sets, preserving their fine-grained semantics while enabling efficient navigation.
results.abstract: GEM is presented, a native indexing framework for multi-vector representations to construct a proximity graph directly over vector sets, preserving their fine-grained semantics while enabling efficient navigation.

results.title: Complementary Document Representations for Information Retrieval
results.abstract: <jats:p>In this paper, we present an approach for combining different document representations to support retrieval systems to deliver similar documents from different views.</jats:p>

results.title: Exploring Vector-Based Methods for Effective Document Retrieval
results.abstract: <jats:p>With the development of online storage services and the internet, people, especially students, often collect numerous documents from the servers. Searching from abundant documents can become a difficult task. With the rapid development of the LLM (Large Language Model) and word embedding technique, people should have the chance to find the information in a new way, which is much more effective. In this paper, with the vectorization of documents and users’ questions with different models,

results.title:
<scp>CameraVQ</scp>
                    : Vector‐Quantized Representations for Monocular Camera Calibration
results.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We present a monocular camera calibration method, dubbed CameraVQ. It reformulates monocular camera calibration as classification over vector‐quantized camera intrinsics. Existing methods based on geometric cues or direct regression often suffer from unstable optimization and poor generalization. In contrast, CameraVQ learns a discrete codebook of camera intrinsics and predicts the latent code from a single image. This discrete formulat

results.title: The Left Spectrum and Irreducible Representations of ‘Small’ Quantized and Classical Rings
results.venue: Noncommutative Algebraic Geometry and Representations of Quantized Algebras

results.title: Visual RAG at Scale: Tile-Level Spatial Pooling for Efficient Multi-Vector Document Retrieval
results.venue: Proceedings of the 49th International ACM SIGIR Conference on Research and Development in Information Retrieval
results.abstract: This work presents Visual RAG Toolkit, a practical system for scaling visual multi-vector retrieval with training-free, model-aware pooling and multi-stage retrieval, and yields a quadratic reduction in vector-to-vector comparisons by reducing stored vectors per page from thousands to dozens.
results.tldr: This work presents Visual RAG Toolkit, a practical system for scaling visual multi-vector retrieval with training-free, model-aware pooling and multi-stage retrieval, and yields a quadratic reduction in vector-to-vector comparisons by reducing stored vectors per page from thousands to dozens.

results.abstract: <jats:p>This paper introduces a novel conceptual framework to support the creation of knowledge representations based on enriched Semantic Vectors, using the classical vector space model approach extended with ontological support. One of the primary research challenges addressed here relates to the process of formalization and representation of document contents, where most existing approaches are limited and only take into account the explicit, word-based information in the document. Vector Quantized Feature Fields for Fast 3D Semantic Lifting
results.tldr: This work generalizes lifting to semantic lifting by incorporating per-view masks that indicate relevant pixels for lifting tasks, and introduces the Vector-Quantized Feature Field, which enables text-driven localized scene editing and significantly improves the efficiency of embodied question answering.
results.abstract: This work generalizes lifting to semantic lifting by incorporating per-view masks that indicate relevant pixels for lifting tasks, and introduces the Vector-Quantized Feature Field, which enables text-driven localized scene editing and significantly improves the efficiency of embodied question answering.

results.title: Document-as-Image Representations Fall Short for Scientific Retrieval
results.tldr: This work introduces ArXivDoc, a new benchmark constructed from the underlying LaTeX sources of scientific papers, and systematically compares text-only, image-based, and multimodal representations across both single-vector and multi-vector retrieval models.
results.abstract: This work introduces ArXivDoc, a new benchmark constructed from the underlying LaTeX sources of scientific papers, and systematically compares text-only, image-based, and multimodal representations across both single-vector and multi-vector retrieval models.

results.title: Stellar: Scalable Multimodal Document Retrieval for Natural Language Queries
results.tldr: Stellar is presented, a scalable multimodal document retrieval framework that stores token-level document embeddings on disk and loads only a small set of candidate embeddings into memory for late interaction and reduces memory overhead and query latency by 1-2 orders of magnitude.
results.abstract: Stellar is presented, a scalable multimodal document retrieval framework that stores token-level document embeddings on disk and loads only a small set of candidate embeddings into memory for late interaction and reduces memory overhead and query latency by 1-2 orders of magnitude.

results.title: Document Analysis, Chat and Comparison Portal Using Advanced Retrieval-Augmented Generation and LLMOps Framework
results.venue: International Scientific Journal of Engineering and Management
results.tldr: This research presents a professional Document Analysis, Chat and Comparison Portal powered by Retrieval-Augmented Generation, semantic search, vector embeddings, and LLMOps deployment practices, which is suitable for enterprise document intelligence applications including legal analysis, academic research, policy, and organizational knowledge management.
results.abstract: This research presents a professional Document Analysis, Chat and Comparison Portal powered by Retrieval-Augmented Generation, semantic search, vector embeddings, and LLMOps deployment practices, which is suitable for enterprise document intelligence applications including legal analysis, academic research, policy, and organizational knowledge management.

results.title: Inference-Free Multimodal Learned Sparse Retrieval for Production-Scale Visual Document Search
results.tldr: V-SPLADE is presented, an inference-free sparse retriever for visual-document retrieval that introduces caption-gated token supervision, a training-only signal that uses VLM-generated captions as lexical cues to activate retrieval-relevant vocabulary dimensions.
results.abstract: V-SPLADE is presented, an inference-free sparse retriever for visual-document retrieval that introduces caption-gated token supervision, a training-only signal that uses VLM-generated captions as lexical cues to activate retrieval-relevant vocabulary dimensions.

results.title: Document Optimization for Black-Box Retrieval via Reinforcement Learning
results.tldr: This work recast document expansion as a document optimization problem: a language model or a vision language model is fine-tuned to transform documents into representations that better align with the expected query distribution under a target retriever, using GRPO with the retriever's ranking improvements as rewards.
results.abstract: This work recast document expansion as a document optimization problem: a language model or a vision language model is fine-tuned to transform documents into representations that better align with the expected query distribution under a target retriever, using GRPO with the retriever's ranking improvements as rewards.

results.title: Semantic Entanglement in Vector-Based Retrieval: A Formal Framework and Context-Conditioned Disentanglement Pipeline for Agentic RAG Systems
results.tldr: This work formalizes entanglement as a model-relative measure of cross-topic overlap in embedding space and defines an Entanglement Index (EI) as a quantitative proxy, and argues that higher EI constrains attainable Top-K retrieval precision under cosine similarity retrieval.
results.abstract: This work formalizes entanglement as a model-relative measure of cross-topic overlap in embedding space and defines an Entanglement Index (EI) as a quantitative proxy, and argues that higher EI constrains attainable Top-K retrieval precision under cosine similarity retrieval.

results.title: Adaptive Hybrid-Kernel Multiple Kernel Learning Support Vector Machine for Text Categorization and Information Retrieval
results.venue: International Journal of Intelligent Information Technologies
results.tldr: Experiments on benchmark text classification and information retrieval datasets demonstrate consistent improvements over strong single-kernel and multiple kernel learning baselines, while kernel approximation techniques maintain scalability with limited performance degradation.
results.abstract: Experiments on benchmark text classification and information retrieval datasets demonstrate consistent improvements over strong single-kernel and multiple kernel learning baselines, while kernel approximation techniques maintain scalability with limited performance degradation.

results.title: Efficient and Effective Multi-Vector Dense Retrieval with EMVB
results.venue: Sistemi Evoluti per Basi di Dati
results.tldr: Efficient Multi-Vector dense retrieval with Bit vectors (EMVB) is introduced, a novel framework for efficient query processing in multi-vector dense retrieval that achieves up to a 2 .
results.abstract: Efficient Multi-Vector dense retrieval with Bit vectors (EMVB) is introduced, a novel framework for efficient query processing in multi-vector dense retrieval that achieves up to a 2 .

results.title: AN INTELLIGENT FOLDER-BASED DOCUMENT CONVERSATIONAL SYSTEM USING LARGE LANGUAGE MODELS AND RETRIEVAL-AUGMENTED GENERATION
results.venue: EPRA international journal of multidisciplinary research
results.tldr: This study proposes and evaluates a document question-answering system whose distinguishing characteristic is awareness of the folder hierarchy in which documents reside, demonstrating the maturity of hybrid generative-retrieval methods for workplace information management.
results.abstract: This study proposes and evaluates a document question-answering system whose distinguishing characteristic is awareness of the folder hierarchy in which documents reside, demonstrating the maturity of hybrid generative-retrieval methods for workplace information management.
